articles.headline: India Successfully Tests Indigenous Hypersonic Missile, Joins Elite Club
articles.explainer:
• India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) successfully conducted the maiden flight test of an indigenously developed hypersonic missile from Abdul Kalam Island, Odisha.
• The test marks a significant leap in India's defence capabilities, placing it among a select few nations possessing this advanced technology.
• Hypersonic missiles travel at speeds exceeding Mach 5, offering enhanced precision, maneuverability, and reduced detection time, posing significant challenges to existing missile defence systems.
• This achievement underscores India's commitment to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) in critical defence technologies and strengthens its strategic deterrence.
gsSubTopics: Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Defence technology
Missile systems
Space and Defence
prelimsPoints: Abdul Kalam Island (formerly Wheeler Island) in Odisha is a key missile test facility.
DRDO is India's premier agency for military research and development.
Hypersonic speed is defined as Mach 5 (five times the speed of sound) or greater.
Hypersonic glide vehicles (HGVs) and Hypersonic cruise missiles (HCMs) are two primary categories of hypersonic weapons.
India previously tested a Hypersonic Technology Demonstrator Vehicle (HSTDV) in 2020.
mainsPoints: Enhances India's strategic deterrence capabilities in a complex geopolitical landscape, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
Boosts 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives, reducing reliance on foreign defence imports and fostering indigenous innovation.
Discuss technological challenges and advancements required, including materials science, propulsion systems (e.g., scramjet engines), and precision guidance.
Examine the impact on regional power balance and the potential for a new arms race, necessitating careful diplomatic engagement.
Analyze the dual-use implications of hypersonic technology for both military and potential civilian applications (e.g., rapid transport).
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of a hypersonic missile launching from a test site.
articles.trivia: The 'Abdul Kalam Island' was renamed in 2015 in honour of former President of India and missile scientist, Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam.

articles.headline: Winter Session of Parliament Commences with Focus on Key Legislations
articles.explainer:
• The Winter Session of the Indian Parliament commenced today, setting the stage for crucial legislative business and debates.
• Key bills related to economic reforms, social welfare, and national security are expected to be introduced and discussed.
• Opposition parties have indicated their intent to raise issues concerning inflation, unemployment, and federal fiscal relations.
• The session is anticipated to be productive, with the government aiming to pass several ordinances and long-pending legislations.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: The President summons each House of Parliament to meet.
There are usually three sessions in a year: Budget, Monsoon, and Winter sessions.
The maximum gap between two sessions of Parliament cannot exceed six months.
A 'session' is the period from the first sitting of a House till its prorogation or dissolution.
An 'ordinance' is a temporary law issued by the President when Parliament is not in session.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of parliamentary sessions in a democratic setup for accountability and law-making.
Analyze the role of the Opposition in ensuring robust debate and scrutinizing government policies during parliamentary proceedings.
Examine challenges faced by parliamentary functioning, such as frequent disruptions, low attendance, and the bypassing of standing committees.
Suggest reforms to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of parliamentary sessions in addressing contemporary issues.
Evaluate the balance between executive urgency (ordinances) and legislative deliberation in the Indian context.
articles.imageDescription: An exterior view of the Parliament House of India.
articles.trivia: The Indian Parliament consists of the President and two Houses: the Council of States (Rajya Sabha) and the House of the People (Lok Sabha).

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its bi-monthly review, opting to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes amidst persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food prices, and a cautious outlook on global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to balance growth impulses with the imperative of price stability.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to closely monitor evolving economic indicators and take appropriate measures as required.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims to keep Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation within 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Open Market Operations (OMOs) are a tool used by RBI to manage liquidity in the economy.
The RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and effectiveness of the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in achieving its dual mandate of price stability and economic growth.
Analyze the impact of interest rate decisions (like maintaining status quo on repo rate) on various sectors of the economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
Examine the challenges faced by monetary policy in India, particularly from supply-side shocks (e.g., food inflation) and global economic volatility.
Evaluate alternative policy tools and unconventional measures that the RBI could deploy in complex economic scenarios.
Critically assess the coordination between fiscal policy (government's budget) and monetary policy (RBI's actions) in managing the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Conclude Joint Maritime Exercise SLINEX 2024
articles.explainer:
• The 12th edition of the bilateral maritime exercise 'SLINEX 2024' between India and Sri Lanka concluded in Visakhapatnam.
• The exercise aimed at enhancing inter-operability, sharing best practices, and strengthening mutual understanding between the two navies.
• It included complex anti-surface, anti-air, and anti-submarine warfare drills, along with humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) operations.
• Such joint exercises are crucial for regional security, fostering cooperation, and addressing common maritime challenges in the Indian Ocean Region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
prelimsPoints: SLINEX is a bilateral maritime exercise between India and Sri Lanka.
It focuses on enhancing mutual understanding and inter-operability between the two navies.
Visakhapatnam is a major naval base on India's eastern coast.
HADR stands for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief.
India has a 'Neighbourhood First' policy, emphasizing strong ties with immediate neighbours.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's maritime cooperation with Sri Lanka in the context of the Indian Ocean Region's geopolitical dynamics.
Analyze how joint military exercises like SLINEX contribute to enhancing regional security and stability, particularly in countering non-traditional threats.
Examine India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its implications for defence and security cooperation with South Asian countries.
Evaluate the role of HADR operations in strengthening bilateral ties and projecting India as a responsible regional power.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in maintaining maritime domain awareness and ensuring freedom of navigation in the IOR.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka participating in a joint exercise.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third largest of the world's oceanic divisions and covers about 20% of the water on Earth's surface.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Implementation of National Education Policy 2020
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• Focus areas included the integration of vocational education, foundational literacy and numeracy, and digital learning initiatives.
• States and Union Territories presented their progress reports and highlighted challenges in aligning state-specific curricula with NEP guidelines.
• The review emphasized accelerating reforms to achieve the NEP's vision of a holistic, flexible, and multidisciplinary education system.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
It aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Key structural changes include 5+3+3+4 curricular structure (ages 3-18).
Emphasis on multidisciplinary education, critical thinking, and experiential learning.
National Research Foundation (NRF) is proposed to fund research across disciplines.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system, such as access, equity, quality, and accountability.
Analyze the key reforms proposed by NEP 2020, including early childhood care, vocational integration, multidisciplinary approach, and digital education.
Examine the challenges in implementing NEP 2020, particularly related to financial resources, teacher training, state-level coordination, and resistance to change.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education under NEP, especially for marginalized and disadvantaged groups.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital platforms in achieving the goals of NEP 2020, particularly in a post-pandemic world.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting engaged in learning.
articles.trivia: The first National Policy on Education was promulgated in 1968, based on the recommendations of the Kothari Commission (1964-66).

articles.headline: India Releases National Report on Climate Change Adaptation Strategies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released its latest national report detailing India's progress and strategies for climate change adaptation.
• The report highlights initiatives in water management, sustainable agriculture, coastal protection, and resilient infrastructure.
• It emphasizes the importance of local-level adaptation planning and community participation in building climate resilience.
• The findings will inform India's updated Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) and position for upcoming international climate negotiations.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Adaptation refers to adjustments in ecological, social, or economic systems in response to actual or expected climatic stimuli and their effects.
Mitigation refers to efforts to reduce or prevent the emission of greenhouse gases.
India's NDCs under the Paris Agreement include reducing emissions intensity of GDP and increasing non-fossil fuel energy capacity.
The National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) outlines eight national missions for climate action.
The Green Climate Fund (GCF) is a financial mechanism under the UNFCCC to support adaptation and mitigation in developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the specific climate change vulnerabilities faced by India and the necessity of robust adaptation strategies across various sectors.
Analyze the key components of India's climate change adaptation framework, including policies, programs, and institutional mechanisms.
Examine the challenges in implementing adaptation measures, such as funding gaps, technological access, inter-state coordination, and capacity building.
Evaluate the role of local communities, traditional knowledge, and nature-based solutions in building effective climate resilience.
Critically assess India's position and contributions to global climate change negotiations, particularly concerning adaptation finance and technology transfer.
articles.imageDescription: A map of India highlighting regions vulnerable to climate change impacts.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest emitter of greenhouse gases globally, but its per capita emissions are significantly lower than many developed nations.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Small Satellite for Earth Observation
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a small satellite aboard the Small Satellite Launch Vehicle (SSLV-D3) from Sriharikota.
• The satellite, named 'Bhuvana-1', is designed for high-resolution Earth observation, focusing on agricultural monitoring and disaster management.
• This mission further validates the SSLV, ISRO's new cost-effective and demand-responsive launch vehicle for smaller payloads.
• The launch reinforces India's growing capabilities in the global small satellite market and its commitment to space-based applications for national development.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO is the primary space agency of India.
SSLV is designed to launch small satellites up to 500 kg to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) and GSLV (Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle) are ISRO's other operational launch vehicles.
Earth observation satellites provide data for various applications like weather forecasting, urban planning, and resource mapping.
Sriharikota, in Andhra Pradesh, hosts India's primary spaceport, the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of India's small satellite launch capabilities (SSLV) in the context of the burgeoning global space market and 'NewSpace' initiatives.
Analyze how Earth observation satellites contribute to national development, specifically in areas like agriculture, disaster management, and environmental monitoring.
Examine the role of ISRO in fostering scientific temper and technological innovation in India, promoting 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in space technology.
Evaluate the economic potential of India's space sector, including private sector participation, satellite manufacturing, and launch services.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in ensuring sustainable and responsible use of outer space, including space debris management.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the SSLV rocket launching into space.
articles.trivia: ISRO holds the world record for launching the most satellites in a single mission (104 satellites in 2017).

articles.headline: Digitalization Project of Ancient Indian Manuscripts Gains Momentum
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced significant progress in the national project to digitalize and preserve ancient Indian manuscripts and archival records.
• This initiative aims to make invaluable historical, scientific, and literary texts accessible to scholars and the public worldwide.
• Advanced imaging and data management technologies are being employed to ensure high-quality digital preservation.
• The project is crucial for safeguarding India's rich cultural heritage and promoting research in Indology and related fields.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: National Mission for Manuscripts (NMM) was launched in 2003 by the Ministry of Culture.
Manuscripts are handwritten documents, often on palm leaves, birch bark, or handmade paper.
Digitalization helps in preserving fragile documents and making them globally accessible.
Major institutions involved include the National Archives of India and various university libraries.
India has one of the largest collections of historical manuscripts in the world.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving and digitalizing ancient Indian manuscripts for understanding India's historical, cultural, and scientific contributions.
Analyze the challenges involved in manuscript preservation, including environmental factors, lack of expertise, and funding, and how digitalization addresses these.
Examine the potential of digital archives to democratize access to knowledge and foster research in Indology, philosophy, science, and literature.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives and public-private partnerships in safeguarding India's tangible and intangible cultural heritage.
Suggest ways to leverage technology not just for preservation, but also for interpretation and popularization of ancient texts among the younger generation.
articles.imageDescription: An ancient Indian manuscript opened, showing intricate script and illustrations.
articles.trivia: The Rigveda, one of the oldest known texts, is recognized by UNESCO in its Memory of the World Register.

articles.headline: India, Pakistan Hold Talks on Indus Water Treaty Issues
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held discussions on various aspects of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) during a meeting of the Permanent Indus Commission.
• The talks focused on technical issues, including the design of hydroelectric projects on the western rivers, and data sharing.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to resolve differences amicably within the framework of the treaty.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, is one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally, surviving decades of geopolitical tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan.
It was brokered by the World Bank.
India got exclusive use of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej).
Pakistan got exclusive use of the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab).
The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is a bilateral commission for implementing and managing the IWT.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and the unique features that have made the Indus Water Treaty a resilient agreement despite India-Pakistan hostilities.
Analyze the current challenges and points of contention regarding the IWT, particularly concerning India's hydroelectric projects on Western Rivers.
Examine the role of international mediation (World Bank) in such transboundary water disputes and its effectiveness.
Evaluate the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the long-term viability and potential modifications of the IWT.
Suggest diplomatic and technical approaches for India and Pakistan to ensure the continued peaceful resolution of water-related issues.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and the distribution of rivers between India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus River is one of the longest rivers in Asia, originating in the Tibetan Plateau.

articles.headline: GST Collections Show Robust Growth in November, Signalling Economic Recovery
articles.explainer:
• Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections for November 2024 recorded a significant year-on-year increase, indicating sustained economic recovery.
• The Finance Ministry attributed the strong performance to increased compliance, festive season demand, and improved economic activity.
• This consistent growth provides a positive outlook for government revenues and fiscal stability.
• The data suggests continued momentum in manufacturing and consumption sectors, crucial for India's economic trajectory.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017.
It is a consumption-based indirect tax levied on the supply of goods and services.
GST subsumed various central and state indirect taxes.
The GST Council is the governing body for GST, chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
There are typically four main GST slabs: 5%, 12%, 18%, and 28%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of robust GST collections as an indicator of India's economic health and formalization of the economy.
Analyze the impact of GST on government revenues, fiscal federalism, and the ease of doing business in India.
Examine the challenges that remain in the GST regime, such as rate rationalization, compliance burden for MSMEs, and addressing revenue shortfalls for states.
Evaluate the role of technology (e.g., e-invoicing, data analytics) in improving GST compliance and preventing tax evasion.
Suggest further reforms to optimize the GST framework for achieving higher economic efficiency and inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing rising GST collection trends over months.
articles.trivia: France was the first country to implement GST (or VAT) in 1954.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Inaugurates Key Infrastructure Project in North East
articles.explainer:
• The Prime Minister inaugurated a multi-modal logistics park and a strategic highway expansion project in a significant boost to infrastructure in the North Eastern Region.
• The projects aim to enhance connectivity, facilitate trade with neighbouring countries, and spur economic development in the region.
• This initiative is part of the government's 'Act East' policy and commitment to bringing the North East into the national mainstream.
• Improved infrastructure is expected to create employment opportunities and attract private investment.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: The North Eastern Region (NER) comprises eight states: Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, and Tripura.
The 'Act East Policy' was launched in 2014, succeeding the 'Look East Policy' of 1991.
Multi-modal logistics parks integrate various modes of transport (road, rail, water, air) for efficient freight movement.
Connectivity projects in NER are crucial for trade with Southeast Asian countries.
The Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DONER) oversees development in the area.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and economic significance of infrastructure development in India's North Eastern Region, particularly in the context of the 'Act East Policy'.
Analyze how improved connectivity (roads, railways, waterways, airports) can address the historical challenges of isolation and underdevelopment in the NER.
Examine the potential of multi-modal logistics parks to transform trade dynamics, reduce logistics costs, and boost economic integration with neighbouring countries.
Evaluate the socio-economic impact of large-scale infrastructure projects on local communities, including employment generation and sustainable development.
Suggest policy measures and investment models to further accelerate infrastructure development and ensure equitable growth across the North East.
articles.imageDescription: The Prime Minister inaugurating a new highway section with a ribbon-cutting ceremony.
articles.trivia: The Brahmaputra River, a major waterway in the North East, is known as Yarlung Tsangpo in Tibet.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Resource Allocation Dispute
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment today, reaffirming the constitutional powers of state governments in matters of natural resource allocation.
• The ruling stemmed from a dispute between a state and the Union government over mining rights and revenue sharing.
• The Court emphasized the principles of cooperative federalism and fiscal autonomy for states.
• This judgment is expected to have significant implications for center-state financial relations and governance of natural resources.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: The Indian Constitution establishes a federal system with a strong unitary bias.
The Seventh Schedule distributes legislative powers between the Union and State governments.
Natural resources like land and mineral rights often fall under the State List.
Cooperative federalism emphasizes cooperation between the center and states for national development.
The Supreme Court is the guardian of the Constitution and resolves center-state disputes.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cooperative and competitive federalism in India, citing instances of center-state disputes over resources and finances.
Analyze the constitutional provisions that delineate the powers of the Union and State governments concerning natural resource management and revenue sharing.
Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the fiscal autonomy of states and its potential to reshape center-state financial relations.
Evaluate the challenges posed by resource-related disputes to the federal fabric of India and suggest mechanisms for effective dispute resolution.
Critically assess the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to federalism and ensuring a balanced distribution of powers.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The term 'federal' is not explicitly used in the Indian Constitution, but Article 1 describes India as a 'Union of States'.

articles.headline: India's G20 Sherpa Attends Key Preparatory Meeting Ahead of Summit
articles.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa participated in a crucial preparatory meeting, reviewing the agenda and drafting the declaration for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions focused on global economic recovery, climate finance, digital public infrastructure, and sustainable development goals.
• India reiterated its commitment to fostering inclusive and action-oriented outcomes from the summit.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus among member nations on pressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: The G20 (Group of Twenty) is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
It works to address major issues related to the global economy, such as international financial stability, climate change mitigation, and sustainable development.
A 'Sherpa' is a personal representative of the head of state or government of a G20 member country.
India hosted the G20 Summit in 2023.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private sector innovation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the G20 as a premier forum for international economic cooperation and its evolving agenda beyond traditional economic issues.
Analyze India's role and objectives within the G20, particularly its emphasis on issues like climate finance, digital public infrastructure, and the voice of the Global South.
Examine the challenges in achieving consensus among G20 members, given diverse national interests and geopolitical tensions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of 'Sherpa' meetings and ministerial discussions in shaping the outcomes of G20 Summits.
Suggest how India can leverage its G20 membership to promote its national interests and contribute to addressing global challenges effectively.
articles.imageDescription: A logo representing the G20 forum with member flags.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

articles.headline: Government Announces Expansion of 'Startup India' Initiative to Tier-2 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry unveiled plans to significantly expand the 'Startup India' initiative, focusing on fostering entrepreneurship in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• New incubation centers, mentorship programs, and seed funding schemes will be launched to support local startups.
• The move aims to decentralize the startup ecosystem, create employment, and leverage untapped talent beyond metropolitan areas.
• This expansion is expected to boost economic growth and innovation across diverse regions of the country.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The 'Startup India' initiative was launched in 2016.
It aims to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in the country.
Key pillars include simplification and handholding, funding support, and industry-academia partnership.
Tier-2 cities are generally those with populations between 50,000 and 100,000.
The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) is the nodal agency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind expanding the 'Startup India' initiative to Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities and its potential impact on regional economic development and job creation.
Analyze the challenges faced by startups in smaller cities compared to metropolitan areas, such as access to funding, talent, and mentorship.
Examine how government policies and schemes like incubation centers, seed funding, and tax incentives can foster a robust entrepreneurial ecosystem in non-metro regions.
Evaluate the role of technology and digital platforms in enabling startups in remote areas to access markets and resources.
Suggest measures to bridge the gap between urban and rural innovation, ensuring inclusive growth of India's startup landscape.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space.
articles.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Reports Significant Progress in Rural Healthcare
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare released a report detailing the achievements of the National Health Mission (NHM) in strengthening rural healthcare infrastructure.
• Key indicators showed improvements in maternal and child health services, reduction in infant mortality rates, and increased access to primary healthcare.
• The mission has played a crucial role in enhancing the capacity of public health facilities and deploying healthcare professionals in remote areas.
• Despite progress, challenges remain in addressing health disparities and ensuring universal healthcare coverage.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by subsuming National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).
Its main programmatic components include Health System Strengthening, Reproductive-Maternal-Neonatal-Child and Adolescent Health (RMNCAH).
ASHA workers are community health volunteers under NHM.
The mission aims to achieve universal access to equitable, affordable & quality healthcare services.
India aims to reduce Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) to 23 by 2025 and Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) to 100 by 2020 (goal already surpassed, new target 70 by 2030).
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and impact of the National Health Mission (NHM) in transforming rural healthcare delivery and improving key health indicators in India.
Analyze the specific interventions under NHM, such as strengthening primary healthcare, promoting institutional deliveries, and deploying community health workers (ASHAs).
Examine the persistent challenges in achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities across different regions and socio-economic groups.
Evaluate the effectiveness of public-private partnerships and the role of technology (e.g., telemedicine, digital health records) in enhancing healthcare access and quality.
Suggest policy recommendations for strengthening the NHM further, focusing on adequate funding, human resource development, and disease prevention strategies.
articles.imageDescription: A rural health worker interacting with women and children in a village.
articles.trivia: India's public health expenditure as a percentage of GDP is among the lowest globally, often around 1.2-1.5%.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Reports Continued Success, Calls for Habitat Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The latest census data from Project Tiger indicates a sustained increase in the tiger population across various reserves in India.
• Wildlife authorities hailed the success of conservation efforts but emphasized the critical need for maintaining and enhancing habitat connectivity.
• Fragmentation of tiger habitats due to developmental projects remains a significant concern, posing threats to genetic diversity and long-term survival.
• The report calls for integrated land-use planning and eco-sensitive development around tiger reserves.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population.
National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) is the statutory body for tiger conservation.
Tiger reserves are designated protected areas for tiger conservation.
Habitat connectivity (wildlife corridors) is crucial for genetic exchange between isolated populations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the success story of Project Tiger in India, highlighting the strategies and policies that led to the increase in tiger population.
Analyze the emerging challenges to tiger conservation, particularly habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching.
Examine the importance of wildlife corridors and landscape-level conservation approaches for ensuring the long-term viability of tiger populations.
Evaluate the economic and ecological benefits of tiger conservation, including its role as an umbrella species for broader biodiversity protection.
Suggest policy interventions and multi-stakeholder collaborations for balancing developmental aspirations with environmental conservation around tiger habitats.
articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage.
articles.trivia: Tigers are apex predators and their presence is an indicator of the health of the ecosystem.

articles.headline: Digital Payments Continue Upward Trend, Boost Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• The latest data from the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) shows sustained growth in digital payment transactions across various platforms.
• UPI (Unified Payments Interface) remains a dominant force, driving financial inclusion and ease of transactions, especially in semi-urban and rural areas.
• The government's push for a 'less-cash' economy and rapid adoption of FinTech solutions are key factors contributing to this trend.
• This growth signifies a deepening of India's digital economy and provides a robust framework for future financial innovation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: UPI is an instant real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
NPCI is an umbrella organisation for operating retail payments and settlement systems in India.
Financial inclusion aims to provide financial services to all sections of society, especially vulnerable groups.
BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) is a UPI-enabled payment application.
RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) and NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) are other electronic payment systems in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative impact of digital payment systems like UPI on India's economy, particularly in fostering financial inclusion and reducing transaction costs.
Analyze the key factors driving the growth of digital payments in India, including government policies, technological advancements, and changing consumer behaviour.
Examine the challenges associated with the rapid expansion of digital payments, such as cybersecurity risks, digital literacy gaps, and infrastructure availability in remote areas.
Evaluate the role of FinTech innovations and regulatory frameworks in shaping the future of India's digital payment landscape.
Suggest measures to further deepen financial inclusion through digital payments, ensuring equitable access and safeguarding consumer interests.
articles.imageDescription: A person making a digital payment using a smartphone.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than any other real-time payment system globally.

articles.headline: Preparations Underway for Constitution Day Celebrations on November 26
articles.explainer:
• Preparations are in full swing across the country for the upcoming Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) on November 26.
• Educational institutions and government bodies are organizing events to commemorate the adoption of the Indian Constitution.
• The day aims to promote constitutional values among citizens, especially the youth, and reaffirm commitment to its principles.
• Various awareness campaigns are planned to highlight the Preamble, Fundamental Rights, and Duties.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) is celebrated on November 26 each year.
On November 26, 1949, the Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution of India.
The Constitution came into full effect on January 26, 1950 (Republic Day).
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar is widely regarded as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'.
The Preamble is considered the soul of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Constitution Day in fostering constitutional literacy and civic engagement among Indian citizens.
Analyze the foundational principles enshrined in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution and their relevance in contemporary India.
Examine the importance of Fundamental Rights and Fundamental Duties in upholding democracy and promoting responsible citizenship.
Evaluate the role of educational institutions and government initiatives in disseminating constitutional values and promoting a culture of constitutionalism.
Reflect on the challenges to constitutional values in modern India and suggest ways to strengthen adherence to its core principles.
articles.imageDescription: A replica of the Indian Constitution's Preamble displayed.
articles.trivia: The original Constitution of India was handwritten in both Hindi and English.

articles.headline: Guru Nanak Jayanti Celebrated with Fervour Across India
articles.explainer:
• Guru Nanak Jayanti, also known as Gurpurab, was celebrated today across India and globally by the Sikh community.
• The auspicious occasion marks the birth anniversary of Guru Nanak Dev Ji, the founder of Sikhism.
• Devotees participated in 'prabhat pheris' (early morning processions), kirtans, and langars (community meals) at gurdwaras.
• The celebrations emphasize Guru Nanak's teachings of universal brotherhood, equality, selfless service, and devotion to one God.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the world will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.— their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: Guru Nanak Dev (1469-1539) was the founder of Sikhism and the first of the ten Sikh Gurus.
His teachings are compiled in the holy scripture Guru Granth Sahib.
Key principles include 'Naam Japna' (meditation on God's name), 'Kirat Karni' (honest living), and 'Vand Chhakna' (sharing earnings).
Langar is a community kitchen in a gurdwara, offering free meals to all, regardless of religion, caste, or gender.
Kartarpur Sahib Corridor connects Dera Baba Nanak shrine in Punjab, India, with Gurdwara Darbar Sahib in Pakistan.
mainsPoints: Discuss the philosophical and social contributions of Guru Nanak Dev Ji to Indian society, particularly his emphasis on equality, universal brotherhood, and selfless service.
Analyze how the teachings of Sikhism promoted social reform and challenged caste discrimination in medieval India.
Examine the cultural significance of festivals like Gurpurab in fostering community spirit, interfaith harmony, and preserving religious traditions.
Evaluate the relevance of Guru Nanak's philosophy in contemporary times, especially in addressing issues of communalism, inequality, and spiritual vacuum.
Discuss the role of religious institutions and community service (e.g., langar) in promoting social welfare and humanitarian values.
articles.imageDescription: Devotees participating in a Prabhat Pheri procession during Guru Nanak Jayanti.
articles.trivia: Guru Nanak Dev Ji travelled extensively, undertaking four major spiritual journeys (Udasis) to spread his message.

articles.headline: New Large-Scale Solar Power Plant Commissioned in Rajasthan
articles.explainer:
• A new large-scale solar power plant, with a capacity of 500 MW, was commissioned today in Rajasthan, boosting India's renewable energy portfolio.
• The plant utilizes advanced photovoltaic technology and is expected to significantly contribute to the state's and nation's clean energy targets.
• This project aligns with India's commitment to reducing carbon emissions and transitioning towards a sustainable energy future.
• Rajasthan, with its abundant solar insolation, continues to be a leader in solar energy generation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Rajasthan has high solar insolation, making it ideal for solar power generation.
India's target is to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was jointly launched by India and France.
Photovoltaic (PV) technology converts sunlight directly into electricity.
Ultra Mega Solar Power Projects are large-scale solar parks.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of renewable energy sources, particularly solar power, in achieving India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the potential of states like Rajasthan in leading India's solar energy revolution, highlighting geographical advantages and policy support.
Examine the challenges in scaling up solar power generation, including land acquisition, grid integration, financing, and technological advancements.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits of large-scale solar projects, such as job creation, rural electrification, and reduced reliance on fossil fuels.
Suggest policy measures and incentives to further accelerate the adoption of solar energy, including rooftop solar, and promote energy storage solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Rows of solar panels stretching across a desert landscape under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar power plants in the world.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Concerns Over Global Hunger Index Methodology
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Women and Child Development reiterated its concerns regarding the methodology and accuracy of the Global Hunger Index (GHI) report.
• The Ministry highlighted methodological flaws, including the use of a small sample size for the 'Child Wasting' indicator and the reliance on outdated data.
• India emphasized its own robust efforts and significant progress in combating malnutrition and food insecurity through various government schemes.
• The government called for a more nuanced and context-sensitive approach to assessing hunger globally.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
prelimsPoints: The Global Hunger Index (GHI) is published annually by Concern Worldwide and Welthungerhilfe.
GHI scores are based on four indicators: Undernourishment, Child Wasting, Child Stunting, and Child Mortality.
Child Wasting refers to low weight for height, indicating acute undernutrition.
Child Stunting refers to low height for age, indicating chronic undernutrition.
Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) is India's flagship program to improve nutritional outcomes.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional nature of hunger and malnutrition in India, and the various government initiatives aimed at addressing these issues.
Analyze India's concerns regarding the methodology of the Global Hunger Index, particularly its indicators and data sources, and suggest improvements for more accurate assessment.
Examine the challenges in combating malnutrition in India, including socio-economic disparities, food access issues, sanitation, and public health infrastructure.
Evaluate the effectiveness of flagship schemes like Poshan Abhiyaan, Mid-Day Meal Scheme, and Public Distribution System (PDS) in improving nutritional outcomes.
Suggest a comprehensive strategy for India to achieve its nutrition targets, integrating food security, health, sanitation, and women's empowerment.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating the components and scoring of the Global Hunger Index.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries globally in terms of the absolute number of stunted and wasted children.

highlights: India achieves a major defence milestone with the successful test of its indigenous hypersonic missile from Abdul Kalam Island.
The Winter Session of Parliament commences, set to deliberate on key legislations and economic reforms.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains the repo rate, citing persistent inflationary concerns and global uncertainties.
India and Sri Lanka conclude the joint maritime exercise 'SLINEX 2024', enhancing regional security cooperation.
Government reviews the implementation of the National Education Policy 2020, focusing on vocational and digital learning integration.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's recent hypersonic missile test, consider the following statements:
1. The missile was tested from Abdul Kalam Island, located off the coast of Odisha.
2. Hypersonic missiles travel at speeds exceeding Mach 10.
3. The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) developed the missile.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Abdul Kalam Island (formerly Wheeler Island) is a key missile test facility. Statement 2 is incorrect. Hypersonic speed is generally defined as Mach 5 or greater, not necessarily Mach 10. Statement 3 is correct. DRDO is India's premier defence research agency.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (a) Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Board of Directors
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body, headed by the RBI Governor, responsible for determining the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020:
1. It replaced the National Policy on Education of 1968.
2. It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure for school education.
3. It emphasizes rote learning over critical thinking.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education. Statement 2 is correct, the new curricular structure is 5+3+3+4. Statement 3 is incorrect; NEP 2020 emphasizes critical thinking, experiential learning, and holistic development, moving away from rote learning.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was brokered by which international organization?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty, signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, was brokered by the World Bank.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India is/are correct?
1. GST is a direct tax levied on income and profits.
2. The GST Council is chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
3. It subsumed various central and state indirect taxes.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; GST is an indirect tax. Statement 2 is correct; the Union Finance Minister is the ex-officio Chairperson of the GST Council. Statement 3 is correct; GST unified many indirect taxes.

mainsMocks.question: India's successful test of an indigenous hypersonic missile marks a significant leap in its defence capabilities. Discuss the strategic implications of hypersonic technology for India's national security and its role in global power dynamics. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology – Indigenization of technology and developing new technology, Defence technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce hypersonic technology and India's achievement. Elaborate on strategic implications like enhanced deterrence, precision strike capability, and challenges to existing defence systems. Discuss its impact on regional balance and India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in defence. Conclude with the need for responsible development.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent judgment on states' rights in natural resource allocation highlights the evolving dynamics of cooperative federalism in India. Analyze the constitutional framework governing center-state relations in resource management and discuss the challenges and opportunities for effective federal governance. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Define cooperative federalism and its relevance in India. Explain constitutional provisions like the Seventh Schedule regarding natural resources. Discuss how judicial pronouncements influence center-state balance. Elaborate on challenges like resource nationalism, fiscal disparities, and environmental concerns. Suggest ways to strengthen cooperative federalism for equitable resource management.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress under the National Health Mission (NHM), India continues to face challenges in achieving universal healthcare coverage, especially in rural areas. Critically evaluate the successes and shortcomings of NHM and suggest a comprehensive strategy to address health disparities and strengthen primary healthcare. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources; Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly introduce NHM and its objectives. Discuss its successes (e.g., IMR/MMR reduction, infrastructure). Critically analyze shortcomings like funding gaps, human resource shortages, quality of care, and urban-rural disparities. Propose a comprehensive strategy including increased public spending, leveraging technology (telemedicine), strengthening Ayushman Bharat, community participation, and focus on preventive health.

quickBytes.text: Hypersonic missiles travel at Mach 5 or more, making them extremely difficult to intercept.
The maximum gap between two sessions of the Indian Parliament cannot exceed six months.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members, with the RBI Governor as chairperson.
SLINEX is a bilateral maritime exercise between India and Sri Lanka, focusing on naval cooperation.
NEP 2020 proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure for school education in India.
India is committed to achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
SSLV is ISRO's Small Satellite Launch Vehicle, designed for cost-effective launch of smaller payloads.
The Indus Waters Treaty (1960) allocates Eastern Rivers to India and Western Rivers to Pakistan.
GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017, subsuming various indirect taxes.
Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) is celebrated on November 26, commemorating its adoption in 1949.

GS1.headline: Digitalization Project of Ancient Indian Manuscripts Gains Momentum
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced significant progress in the national project to digitalize and preserve ancient Indian manuscripts and archival records.
• This initiative aims to make invaluable historical, scientific, and literary texts accessible to scholars and the public worldwide.
• Advanced imaging and data management technologies are being employed to ensure high-quality digital preservation.
• The project is crucial for safeguarding India's rich cultural heritage and promoting research in Indology and related fields.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: National Mission for Manuscripts (NMM) was launched in 2003 by the Ministry of Culture.
Manuscripts are handwritten documents, often on palm leaves, birch bark, or handmade paper.
Digitalization helps in preserving fragile documents and making them globally accessible.
Major institutions involved include the National Archives of India and various university libraries.
India has one of the largest collections of historical manuscripts in the world.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving and digitalizing ancient Indian manuscripts for understanding India's historical, cultural, and scientific contributions.
Analyze the challenges involved in manuscript preservation, including environmental factors, lack of expertise, and funding, and how digitalization addresses these.
Examine the potential of digital archives to democratize access to knowledge and foster research in Indology, philosophy, science, and literature.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives and public-private partnerships in safeguarding India's tangible and intangible cultural heritage.
Suggest ways to leverage technology not just for preservation, but also for interpretation and popularization of ancient texts among the younger generation.
GS1.imageDescription: An ancient Indian manuscript opened, showing intricate script and illustrations.
GS1.trivia: The Rigveda, one of the oldest known texts, is recognized by UNESCO in its Memory of the World Register.
